title: Alkaline-SDS cell lysis of microbes with acetone protein precipitation for proteomic sample preparation in 96-well plate format
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cbctv","text":"This high-throughput protocol details the steps to extract protein from Gram-negative bacteria, Gram-positive bacteria, or non-filamentous fungi in 96-well plate format for quantitative proteomic workflows. This protocol uses a bench-top automated liquid dispenser but the volumes and times also apply to manual and multi-channel pipetter use. This protocol is designed for lab-based, culture conditions and synthetic community experiments where complex sample matrices are minimized. Additional sample preservation and/or protein extraction methods may be required for environmental samples (e.g., feces, soil) to minimize protein degradation and maintain sample integrity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3dh20","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"93nn5","text":"This protocol works best as part of a high-throughput proteomic sample preparation workflow with:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4cri8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70g96","text":"Automated Protein Quantitation with the Biomek-FX liquid handler system","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":71}],"data":{}},{"key":"66png","text":"\nand","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgpjb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2f6vp","text":"Automated Protein Normalization and Tryptic Digestion on a Biomek-NX Liquid Handler System","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":90}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"41459580704411ED8F9B0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/automated-protein-quantitation-with-the-biomek-fx-bsyynfxw"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"414595CC704411ED8F9B0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/automated-protein-normalization-and-tryptic-digest-buypnxvn"}}}}

This protocol works in larger formats (e.g., 1.7 mL Eppendorf tubes) too.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9181g","text":"For this protocol you will need:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ao12","text":"- a bench-top automated liquid dispenser (e.g., Formulatrix Mantis) or manual/multi-channel pipetters","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nkon","text":"- an Eppendorf 5810R centrifuge with S-4-104 rotor or similar centrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ppfj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5jld1","text":"Mix at least 3 mL of NaOH/SDS buffer for final concentrations of:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnjpl","text":"- 200 mM NaOH","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63jbq","text":"- 1% SDS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88g6p","text":"or use Qiagen Lysis Buffer P2 (Qiagen, Cat.#19052)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"an9a6","text":"Acetone is used in this protocol so please follow the appropriate safety guidelines for handling and disposing of non-halogenated solvents at your institution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bb1j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"benjn","text":"Sodium Hydroxide is a HIGHLY CORROSIVE CHEMICAL and contact can severely irritate and burn the skin and eyes with possible eye damage. Inhaling Sodium Hydroxide can irritate the lungs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9i8i5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h896","text":"Wear gloves and appropriate PPE for safety and to minimize contamination of samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Start with 10 µL of cells per well a non-skirted PCR plate (Thermo Scientific, Cat.#AB0600). Plate of cell pellets When processing larger amounts of biomass increase reagent volumes accordingly. This protocol works in larger formats (e.g., 1.7 mL Eppendorf tubes) too.
Resuspend the cell pellet in lysis buffer on a plate mixer. Ensure proper mixing of the cell lysis buffer and the cell pellet. Plate mixer Cells resuspended in lysis buffer
Add 2.75 µL 1 Molarity (M), Hydrochloric acid (Sigma Cat.#320331) to each well.
Add 25 µL, 100 mM Ammonium bicarbonate (VWR Scientific Cat.#BJ40867-50G) and 0.5 µL, Benzonase nuclease (Millipore Cat.#70746) to each well.
Mix thoroughly on the plate mixer. Cell lysate after benzonase treatment
Add 200 µL, 100% Acetone (Sigma Cat.#179124) to each well and let sit at room temperature for 5 minutes. Protein precipitation after mixing in 100% acetone
Add 60 µL of 100 millimolar (mM), Ammonium bicarbonate in 10% Methanol to each well to resuspend protein pellet. Protein pellet resuspended in 60 uL of Ammonium bicarbonate in 10% Methanol
Remove supernatant. Protein pellet after centrifugation and removal of supernatant
Wash protein pellet twice using 150 µL, 80% Acetone (Sigma Cat.#179124) . Increase the number of washes depending on the sample matrix background.
Store at -20 °C until ready for Automated Protein Quantitation with the Biomek-FX liquid handler system .
Add 25 µL of alkaline-SDS cell lysis buffer (200 mM NaOH, 1% SDS) to each well.
creator: Jennifer Gin
